title: Lockbox Filing Information | USCIS
content:
## Lockbox Filing Information
This page provides additional information on filing your form with a USCIS lockbox.

#### Submitting Your Form
For tips on filing applications, petitions, or requests with USCIS, please visit our Tips for Filing Forms By Mail page.
For information on requesting a fee waiver, please visit our Additional Information on Filing a Fee Waiver page, and our Form I-912, Request for Fee Waiver , page.

#### If You Sent Your Form to the Wrong Filing Location
If you mailed your form to an incorrect lockbox location, we may forward your submission to the correct processing location. However, if your form is not processed by a lockbox, we will return your package to the address provided on your form.
If you believe your package has been lost, please contact your carrier service. If you have questions about your submission after 30 business days of processing, you may email Lockbox Support at lockboxsupport@uscis.dhs.gov for a review of your submission.

#### After We Receive Your Form
When staff at our lockbox receive your package, we will:
- Evaluate data from scanned images against acceptance criteria;
- Verify that you submitted the correct fee;
- Accept or reject forms and related fees based on current regulations and policies;
- Deposit payments to the U.S. Treasury;
- Send receipt notices for accepted forms to you (and your representative, if applicable);
- Return rejected forms to you (or your representative, if applicable); and
- Send your package to the appropriate USCIS service center or field office for further processing.
Our Case Resolution Units adjudicate fee waiver requests and, whenever possible, resolve issues with form packages before sending them to a USCIS office for further processing.

#### If We Lose Your Documents
If you or your representative believe that a lockbox lost all or part of your package, please email Lockbox Support at lockboxsupport@uscis.dhs.gov and we will investigate.
We will reject your form if it is not properly completed; if the form has an invalid or missing signature; if it is completed using an outdated edition of the form; or if the form pages are from different editions. All pages of the forms that you file must be completed with the same, current edition of the form or we will reject it. The date on the bottom of each form is the edition date. You can check the USCIS webpage for that form to verify whether it is an acceptable edition when you are filing it.
Our lockbox will also reject your form if you do not correctly complete your electronic payment form (Form G-1450 or Form G-1650), or if the payment amount is incorrect. For more information on completing your payment form properly, visit our Filing Fees webpage.
In fiscal year 2025, our lockbox locations processed more than 10 million applications and rejected 11% of them. The most common reason for rejection is that benefit requestors did not submit the correct fee amount associated with their benefit requests.  Use our Fee Calculator to help you determine the correct payment for the benefit you are requesting.
Our lockbox accept forms with information that is deemed critical to acceptance of the form. This information is critical because it is used to process our business rules, such as reasons for filing or eligibility categories, or it is required by USCIS case management systems.

#### Returning Rejected Forms
If we reject your form, we will send you a rejection notice. If you filed your form with a valid Form G-28, Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative , we will return it to the attorney or representative on file and send a rejection notice to both you and your attorney or representative.
If your Form G-28 is not valid (for example, it is missing a signature), we will not honor it and send all correspondence directly to you.
Please see our Filing Your Form G-28 page for additional filing tips.

#### Trouble Contacting Us
If we have not responded to an email from your attorney or representative within 30 days of receiving your package,  you may email us at lockboxsupport@uscis.dhs.gov and we will answer your email as soon as possible. Please include the form number, receipt number, petitioner or applicant name (or both), and mailing address. Do not include a Social Security Number in the email.
